seed: safety vs exposure; pressure_vs_release; split_vertical; echo_right; braid against ripple; pressure_border; thin_vs_heavy; vessel; softness_severity | abstract variation request 502; target batch count 500
title: Echoing Vulnerability
reasoning: This composition evokes the emotional tension between safety and exposure, illustrated through a carefully chosen palette and contrasting patterns. The colors ‘shelter_green’ and ‘warm_brown’ represent the comforting aspects of safety, while ‘open_blue’ and ‘cold_white’ embody the starkness of exposure. The pattern of dots serves to suggest the unpredictability of exposure, while halo effects of the warmer colors create a sense of soft enclosure. The layout presents a ‘broken_frame’ where distinct color breaks suggest leaking emotion. The main collision occurs in the center, where more vibrant colors interact with neutral tones, portraying the delicate balance between vulnerability and protection. The use of negative space further emphasizes the emotional contrast, allowing for quiet reflection against denser color fields.

layout.name: broken_frame
layout.description: A border nearly encloses the square but has deliberate breaks where emotion leaks through.

composition.name: exposure_vs_shelter
composition.idea: A pale exposed center is either protected by a frame or left open to incoming marks.

referenceExample.title: Static Around a Bridge
referenceExample.pattern: cold broken islands interrupted by a warm connecting channel

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
